source_name: Code4rena Dyad Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: Flash loan protection mechanism can be bypassed via self-liquidations
summary: The protocol implements a flash-loan manipulation protection mechanism with the idToBlockOfLastDeposit variable. This values is set to the current block number during a deposit, and is checked during a withdrawal. If the system detects a deposit and withdrawal in the same block, the system reverts the transaction.
root_cause: flash-loan guard is tied to deposit block tracking but self-liquidation provides an alternate same-block path around the protection
broken_invariants: the vault path must not allow the condition described by "Flash loan protection mechanism can be bypassed via self-liquidations" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
users must retain a reliable exit path unless an explicitly designed and validated pause or restriction is active
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: The protocol implements a flash-loan manipulation protection mechanism with the idToBlockOfLastDeposit variable. This values is set to the current block number during a deposit, and is checked during a withdrawal. If the system detects a deposit and withdrawal in the same block, the system reverts the transaction.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: The flashloan protection can be bypassed. MEV liquidation bots rely on flashloans to carryout liquidations, so there isn’t a very good way to prevent this attack vector. Making the price of kerosene less manipulatable is a good way to lower this attack chance. However, the system will still be open to flashloan deposits via liquidations. Incorporating a mint fee will also help mitigate this vector, since the attacker will have a higher cost to manipulate the system.
